name: Zeus (primary deity)
shortDescription: Zeus is the king of the gods in Greek mythology, ruling from Mount Olympus. He is associated with the sky, thunder, lightning, law, order, and justice.
description: Zeus is the supreme ruler of the Olympian gods in Greek mythology. He is the god of the sky, thunder, lightning, law, order, and justice. As the son of Cronus and Rhea, he was saved from being swallowed by his father (who feared a prophecy that one of his children would overthrow him) by his mother, who hid him in a cave on Mount Ida in Crete. When he reached adulthood, Zeus overthrew Cronus and the Titans, establishing himself as the king of the gods. Zeus is often depicted wielding a thunderbolt, his primary weapon. He is known for his numerous affairs with both goddesses and mortal women, resulting in many divine and heroic offspring. His reign is characterized by both power and a constant struggle to maintain order amidst the machinations of gods and mortals. Zeus represents the ultimate authority in the Greek pantheon and embodies the concepts of justice, hospitality, and divine power. He is central to many myths and legends, shaping the course of heroic narratives and divine conflicts. His role extended beyond simply being a powerful king, he was seen as the protector of suppliants and enforcer of oaths, making him a central figure in the moral and social fabric of ancient Greek society.
